report_name: Lawmakers Raise Alarm Over CISA's Operational Readiness Due to Critical Staffing Shortages

threat_actors.name: Salt Typhoon
threat_actors.description: Mentioned as an example of a major cyber incident that CISA's reduced staff would be strained to respond to.

identities.name: Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA)
identities.description: The primary U.S. agency affected by staffing shortages, facing a potential degradation of its ability to perform its mission to defend against cyber threats.

identities.name: Department of Government Efficiency (DOGE)
identities.description: The U.S. government department that mandated workforce reductions at CISA.

identities.name: U.S. Federal Government
identities.description: The broader entity whose security is potentially weakened due to CISA's reduced capacity, specifically federal civilian executive branch (FCEB) networks.

identities.name: U.S. Critical Infrastructure
identities.description: The 16 critical infrastructure sectors that depend on CISA for threat intelligence and incident response coordination are at increased risk due to the agency's staffing shortages.

identities.name: Mark Warner
identities.description: U.S. Senator who publicly warned that CISA's staffing shortages put U.S. homeland security and national defense at risk.

identities.name: Gary Peters
identities.description: U.S. Senator who noted that a weakened CISA directly translates to a more vulnerable homeland.
